_comment: Per-page primary interaction probes. Authored in task 11c (deferred from task 9). Each entry maps a manifest page id to ONE primary interaction the CISO persona can perform. PASS = the interaction triggers without an uncaught console error and the expected DOM signal (a new element, a class change, an XHR firing) is observed. FAIL = a console error fires OR no DOM signal is observed within the timeout. Severity is 'medium' by default. Pages where no interactive element is sensible to probe in a deployed-env smoke (page is purely read-only, requires a specific data row to be loaded, or would write to backend state) are encoded with interaction_id: null and a reason. Those pages are skipped at runtime with the reason recorded in results.json so the coverage matrix shows the gap explicitly rather than silently omitting them. Selectors are kept loose (role-based or text-based) to survive minor Tailwind class shuffles. Severity classifications: 'medium' applies when an interaction is expected to work and silently breaking it would degrade the demo; 'low' for purely cosmetic ones. The spec at e2e/tests/interactions.spec.js iterates this map. Note: signin and the four backend-write-heavy interactions are intentionally null — see per-entry reason.

dashboard.description: Click the 'Open findings' / Findings card on the dashboard to navigate into /findings.

findings.description: Click the first findings table row to open the finding detail.

finding-detail.reason: Direct navigation to /findings/:id with the literal ':id' placeholder lands on a not-found shell; there is no stable interactive element without a real finding id from a prior /findings list call. Covered by the row-click follow-through from the 'findings' interaction in a future cross-page test if needed.

heatmap.description: Click the first heatmap domain tile to drill into its findings.

actions.description: Click the first action card or row to expand its details panel. Does NOT click approve/reject/execute (those mutate backend state — covered in the auth-abuse and fuzz layers).

governance.description: Click the first policy row in the Governance table.

audit.description: Click a filter chip or column header on the Audit Logs page (filter, not row, because rows are immutable log entries).

analyst.reason: Sending a chat prompt here would cost Bedrock tokens against the LLM layer's budget and would write a session row to DDB. The /analyst chat is covered by the LLM red-team layer at task 17. Mounting the page is already covered by e2e.page.analyst.ciso (task 9).

llm-control.description: Click a tab/toggle on the LLM Control Panel (read-only UI; no backend mutation).

pipeline.description: Click a pipeline stage card to expand details.

mcp-chat.description: Click the second MCP server in the sidebar to switch the active server. Does NOT send a chat prompt (that is covered by e2e.mcp-chat.sidebar-cosmetic).

token-usage.description: Click a Governance/Token Tracking tab or filter to change the chart range. Read-only — no backend mutation.

personas.description: Click a persona card on the demo/info Personas page.

settings.description: Click a settings section tab (Profile / Session / Theme).

integrations.description: Type into the connector-catalog search input on the Integrations marketplace page. Read-only filter; no backend mutation.

signin.reason: Authenticated personas hitting /signin are redirected away (Navigate to='/' replace). There is no interactive element on the page for an authenticated persona. The Cognito Hosted UI flow is exercised by the no-auth project's cognito-hosted-ui.spec.js.

spa-root.reason: Synthetic sentinel page in the manifest (synthetic: true). Not a real React page — it represents the index.html + linked JS bundles for Block D bundle scans. No interactive element to probe; bundle-scan coverage lives in the auth/headers layers.
